scope: Scene 1 archetype re-anchor only. Do not auto-apply scenes 2-5. Banking and wealth-discovery flagged 'follow-up' because they need iframe re-routing in addition to copy.
rationale: 16 of 19 FINS/HLS/PS stories opened with 'sender submits intake at the Agreement Desk' — a forced inbound-mailroom framing that doesn't match how the work actually happens. This pass re-anchors Scene 1 to one of four archetypes per story: A (Sender Webform + Maestro), B (Self-Service Webform + Maestro), C (Inbound Agreement Desk - the genuine inbound case), or D (Workspaces-Led - non-linear engagements). Tag, head, and beat-1 head are rewritten; lede first paragraph is rewritten to lead with the actual ignition (form submit, system event, customer tap). Beats 2-5 left untouched intentionally so review can happen at archetype level before deeper copy passes.

wealth.rationale: Internal compliance head fills a webform — Maestro fires the dynamic IMA package. Not an Agreement Desk inbox.

edits.value: SCENE 1 OF 5 · INTAKE · SENDER WEBFORM
Priya kicks off Aster Capital's onboarding from a Hillside intake webform.

Priya Das, Head of Compliance at Hillside Advisors, fills out the new-engagement webform for Aster Capital — entity type, AUM, mandate, custodian.

The moment she submits, Maestro assembles the IMA package dynamically from those selections. The rest of this story runs on its own.
Priya completes the Aster Capital engagement webform.

banking.rationale: $2.5M facility with parallel credit-officer routing and a four-doc package is non-linear. Should open in a Workspaces deal room with all parties present; Webforms+Maestro fire inside the Workspace later. Iframe target also needs to change — flagged as follow-up, not auto-submit.

edits.value: SCENE 1 OF 5 · WORKSPACE · DEAL ROOM
Raj opens a deal room for the Atlas Manufacturing facility.

Raj Malhotra, Commercial Relationship Manager at Meridian, opens a Workspace for Atlas Manufacturing's $2.5M working-capital facility — credit officer Sofia Kim, Atlas's CFO Diana, and Meridian's underwriter all present from minute one.

From inside the deal room, Maestro fires the four-document package: loan agreement, security agreement, UCC-1, personal guaranty.
Raj opens the Atlas Manufacturing deal room.

insurance.rationale: Internal underwriter fills a binder webform — Maestro generates the HO3 package. Not an Agreement Desk inbox.

edits.value: SCENE 1 OF 5 · INTAKE · SENDER WEBFORM
Marisol issues the HO3 binder for the Ramirez family from Sentinel's underwriting webform.

Marisol Vega, Sentinel underwriter, fills out the binder webform for the Ramirez family's new homeowners policy at 412 Briar Lane — coverage limits, deductible, mortgagee, payment plan.

The moment she submits, Maestro assembles the HO3 package — application, binder, declarations, mortgagee notice — and routes to Elena Ramirez.
Marisol completes the Ramirez binder webform.

insurance-life.rationale: Death notices genuinely arrive from outside (funeral home, state vital records, family). This is the rare valid Agreement Desk inbound case — keep the archetype, just lead the lede with the inbound event rather than Camille 'opening' it.

edits.value: SCENE 1 OF 5 · INTAKE · INBOUND
A death notice for Marcus Whitfield arrives at Beacon's Agreement Desk.

A death notice for Marcus Whitfield — $750k whole-life policy holder — arrives at Beacon Mutual's Agreement Desk from the family's funeral director.

The Desk parses it, matches the policy, and routes the file to Camille Boutros in Claims. The rest of the workflow runs on its own.
Beacon's Desk receives the Whitfield death notice.

insurance-pc.rationale: FNOL is loss-event-triggered: customer self-files (mobile app / phone / partner submission) and field adjuster picks up. Lead Scene 1 with Priya's FNOL submission, then Andre triages.

edits.value: SCENE 1 OF 5 · INTAKE · SELF-SERVICE FNOL
Priya files an FNOL on Northgate's mobile app the morning after the I-78 incident.

Yesterday at 04:12 a Class-7 box truck struck a utility pole on I-78. By morning Priya Patel, Patel Logistics' fleet manager, files the first notice of loss from Northgate Mutual's mobile app — vehicle, driver, location, photos.

The submission lands in Northgate's claims queue. Maestro triggers the FNOL package; Andre Sokolov picks up as field adjuster.
Priya files the FNOL from the Northgate mobile app.

banking-deposits.rationale: Reframed 2026-04-27. Canonical = Account Opening (sender-led, A-trig: marketing-funnel→Maestro fires the joint-account package). Account Maintenance moves to ?usecase=auth-fabric as Archetype B (self-service inside the authenticated portal). Both demos now have a clean shape distinction: Opening leads with Maestro's marketing-trigger composition; Maintenance leads with CLEAR-at-the-door step-up on a sensitive customer-initiated change. The previous B-self-service framing for canonical conflated the two and left no room for the maintenance flow.

edits.value: SCENE 1 OF 5 · INTAKE · MAESTRO-TRIGGERED
Cedar's pre-approved household offer fires Maestro; the joint-account package composes itself.

Cedar Federal's marketing funnel surfaces a pre-approved household upgrade to Marcus and Adaeze Williams — joint checking + high-yield savings, terms tailored to their existing-member profile.

Marcus taps the offer. Maestro fires on the capture and assembles the new-account package — pulling his membership data, generating the joint application pre-filled with the household, and queuing Adaeze's identity-proofing step before either signer sees a screen.
Cedar's marketing funnel surfaces the pre-approved offer.

wealth-discovery.rationale: Multi-account onboarding (IRA rollover + family trust + advisory) with co-trustee is too parallel for a webform-and-package opening. Should open in a Workspace relationship room. Iframe target needs to change — flagged as follow-up, not auto-submit.

edits.value: SCENE 1 OF 5 · WORKSPACE · DEAL ROOM
Reggie opens a relationship Workspace for the Holcomb family.

Reggie Adesanya, Senior Financial Advisor at Cypress Wealth, finishes a discovery session with the Holcomb family and opens their relationship Workspace — Walter, Esther, the co-trustee, and Cypress operations all present from the start.

From inside the Workspace, Maestro fires the multi-account package: IRA rollover, joint, family trust, and the advisory agreement.
Reggie opens the Holcomb family Workspace.

provider.rationale: Modern provider intake is patient-portal pre-reg, not coordinator-side packet assembly. Jordan should be the actor in Scene 1; Daniela appears in Workspaces.

edits.value: SCENE 1 OF 5 · INTAKE · SELF-SERVICE PORTAL
Jordan completes new-patient pre-registration in Riverside's patient portal.

Jordan Kim opens Riverside Health's patient portal on a phone the night before their first appointment and completes pre-registration — demographics, insurance card photo, reason for visit, history.

The submission lands in Riverside's intake queue. Maestro assembles the patient packet; Daniela Okafor reviews from Workspaces.
Jordan pre-registers in Riverside's patient portal.

lifesciences.rationale: Sponsor-initiated site activation is exactly Archetype A. Just relabel and tighten — Ethan really is filling a study-config webform that fires Maestro to fan out CTA + budget + 1572 + IRB.

edits.value: SCENE 1 OF 5 · INTAKE · SENDER WEBFORM
Ethan configures site activation for ARC from Helix's study webform.

Ethan Park, Clinical Study Manager at Helix, opens the site-activation webform for Austin Research Clinic on the HX-2104 trial — protocol version, indication, IRB, PI, budget tier.

Maestro fans out the activation packet from his selections: CTA, budget, FDA-1572, IRB approval, financial disclosure.
Ethan completes the ARC site-activation webform.

payor.rationale: Member self-service via marketplace is the dominant enrollment pattern. Marcus should be the actor in Scene 1; Brenda appears in Workspaces for triage.

edits.value: SCENE 1 OF 5 · INTAKE · SELF-SERVICE MARKETPLACE
Marcus enrolls in Unity's Silver HMO from the marketplace.

Marcus Chen compares plans on Unity Health Plan's marketplace and selects the Silver HMO — household composition, household income, PCP preference.

The application lands in Unity's enrollment queue. Maestro assembles the enrollment packet; Brenda Estevez in Member Services reviews from Workspaces.
Marcus selects the Silver HMO from Unity's marketplace.

provider-roi.rationale: External requester (Silver Mountain Disability) genuinely initiates inbound to Catalina HIM. Right archetype — just lead with the inbound request arriving, not Renata 'opening.'

edits.value: SCENE 1 OF 5 · INTAKE · INBOUND
A records request for Eric Tan arrives from Silver Mountain Disability.

A release-of-information request arrives at Catalina Medical's Agreement Desk from Silver Mountain Disability — they're supporting Eric Tan's short-term claim and need the last 18 months of records.

The Desk parses the request, matches the patient, and routes to Renata Voss in HIM. The consent packet generates automatically.
Catalina's Desk receives the Silver Mountain ROI request.

fedgov.rationale: UI claims are claimant-self-filed via state portal. 'Arrives at the Department of Labor's desk' is paper-era language for a digital flow.

edits.value: SCENE 1 OF 5 · INTAKE · SELF-SERVICE PORTAL
Taylor files a UI claim through the state's claimant portal.

Taylor Nguyen opens the state UI portal and files a new unemployment claim — last employer, separation reason, weekly availability, direct deposit.

The claim lands in DOL's adjudication queue. Maestro assembles the determination packet; the assigned adjudicator picks up from Workspaces.
Taylor files the UI claim from the state portal.

slgov.rationale: Modern permitting is applicant-self-service via portal. Marissa is the actual sender; Dean is the back-office reviewer.

edits.value: SCENE 1 OF 5 · INTAKE · SELF-SERVICE PERMIT PORTAL
Marissa applies for the 1420 Oak Street building permit through Springfield's portal.

Marissa Rivera, owner of 1420 Oak Street, applies for a residential building permit through Springfield's permit portal — scope of work, contractor, plans, fees.

The application lands in Community Development's queue. Maestro assembles the permit packet; Dean Okonkwo reviews from Workspaces.
Marissa submits the 1420 Oak Street permit application.

slgov-311.rationale: Body of S1 is right (Maria scans QR, citizen self-service). Head says 'Marisol opens' which contradicts. Lead with Maria's scan.

edits.value: SCENE 1 OF 5 · INTAKE · SELF-SERVICE QR
Maria scans a 311 QR code at 1124 Cedar Ave to report a pothole.

Maria Cortez scans an on-site 311 QR code at 1124 Cedar Ave and submits a service request — pothole the size of a microwave, with a photo and the auto-attached GPS location.

The request lands in Greenhaven Public Works' dispatch queue. Marisol Tran assigns Wesley's crew; SMS updates flow back to Maria.

slgov-benefits.rationale: Already correct shape — only the scene tag needs updating to the new self-service convention.

edits.value: SCENE 1 OF 5 · INTAKE · SELF-SERVICE MOBILE

slgov-recertification.rationale: Recert windows auto-fire — no human 'opens' anything. S1 should lead with Maestro detecting the renewal date, not Asha clicking. This is the cleanest Maestro-as-orchestrator moment in the demo set.

edits.value: SCENE 1 OF 5 · INTAKE · MAESTRO-TRIGGERED
Cascade's recertification window fires for Diane Park.

Maestro detects that Diane Park's annual Medicaid recertification window opens 60 days out — single, age 67, on Medicaid for 11 months following retirement.

No one clicks anything. Maestro pre-fills the recert packet from Cascade's system of record and routes it to Diane's preferred channel; Asha Patel monitors from Workspaces.
Maestro fires Diane's annual recertification window.

slgov-vendor-compliance.rationale: Beat 1 already says 'Maestro detects Apex's expiring compliance certs' — that's right. Tag and head still soften the system-event framing. Lead the head with the Maestro detection.

edits.value: SCENE 1 OF 5 · INTAKE · MAESTRO-TRIGGERED
Maestro detects Apex Logistics' expiring COI and fires the recert.

Maestro detects two compliance certifications expiring in 30 days for Apex Logistics — Class-A trucking vendor, $4.2M annual contract value with Ridgeview Procurement.

The recert packet pre-fills from the active contract and the previous year's submission. Olivia Mendez reviews from Workspaces.

slgov-employee-onboarding.rationale: Beat 1 already says 'Background check clears — Marcus's onboarding triggers' — that's the system event. Scene head still leads with Karina opening. Lead the head with the BG-check event.

edits.value: SCENE 1 OF 5 · INTAKE · MAESTRO-TRIGGERED
Marcus's background check clears — Maestro fires onboarding for the new caseworker.

Maestro receives the cleared background check for Marcus Lee, newly hired Caseworker I at Cascade County, with a start date in 9 days.

The onboarding packet pre-fills from the offer + HRIS: I-9, W-4, benefits enrollment, equipment request, training assignments. Karina Yusuf monitors from Workspaces.

slgov-licensing.rationale: Beat 1 already says 'Mei submits the application' — Mei is the sender. Modern PS licensing is applicant-portal-driven. Lead Scene 1 with Mei in the portal.

edits.value: SCENE 1 OF 5 · INTAKE · SELF-SERVICE LICENSING PORTAL
Mei applies for the Avalon Café restaurant license through Northbrook's portal.

Mei Ortega applies for a restaurant license through Northbrook Licensing's applicant portal — first café at 88 Riverbend Ave, capacity 38 seats, full kitchen.

The application lands in the licensing queue. Maestro assembles the license packet — application, attestation, fee authorization, inspection checklist; Trevor Bowman reviews from Workspaces.
Mei submits the Avalon Café license application.

education.rationale: Modern student onboarding is portal-driven self-service — students complete enrollment, financial aid acceptance, housing, and tuition agreements online. Jamal triages and reviews from Workspaces, not as the back-office packet assembler.

edits.value: SCENE 1 OF 5 · INTAKE · SELF-SERVICE STUDENT PORTAL
Priya completes new-student onboarding through Maple Ridge's student portal.

Priya Shah opens Maple Ridge's student portal the week before move-in and completes new-student onboarding — enrollment selections, financial aid acceptance, housing preferences, immunization records.

The submission lands in the admissions queue. Maestro assembles the four-document onboarding packet; Jamal Rivers in admissions reviews from Workspaces.
Priya completes onboarding from Maple Ridge's student portal.

nonprofit.rationale: Asha is genuinely the internal sender (Harbor program officer configuring the grant package). Just the Agreement Desk framing is forced — pure relabel from inbound inbox to sender webform. Maestro is firing Asha's selections to assemble the grantee package; Agreement Desk in the demo is the proxy that visualizes the fan-out.

edits.value: SCENE 1 OF 5 · INTAKE · SENDER WEBFORM
Asha configures the Safe Streets grant package from Harbor's grant-config webform.

Asha Morgan, Program Officer at Harbor Foundation, fills out the new-grant webform for Safe Streets Initiative — $120k general operating, 12-month period, quarterly reporting, impact framework selected.

The moment she submits, Maestro assembles the grantee package: grant agreement + MOU + W-9 + reporting schedule. 501(c)(3) verification and authorized-signatory confirmation fire in parallel.
Asha completes the Safe Streets grant-config webform.
